summary: Add model + prompt_version provenance to staged_edges; swap propose_edges default to Mistral; backfill historical rows
description:
Two coupled changes, one ticket:

1. ALTER staged_edges to add model TEXT and prompt_version TEXT columns, mirroring the staged_tags pattern from todo:20891195 (P1.3 — tag_concepts/seed_tags write provenance on every insert). Update propose_edges.upsert_staged_edge() to write both fields on every INSERT going forward. Bump UNIQUE constraint from (source_chunk, target_chunk) to (source_chunk, target_chunk, model, prompt_version) so multi-model A/B rows can coexist for the same pair.

2. Swap scripts/propose_edges.py:240 default --model from "Carnice-27b-Q4_K_M.gguf" to "Mistral-Small-3.2-24B-Instruct-2506-UD-Q5_K_XL.gguf". This matches the user's actual usage (run-mistral.sh exists, runs the right model with the right sampling defaults; Carnice was a leftover from another project).

3. Backfill the existing ~5963 staged_edges rows with model = "Mistral-Small-3.2-24B-Instruct-2506-UD-Q5_K_XL.gguf" and prompt_version = "v1" — per the user's recollection that Mistral produced this batch. v1 names the current SYSTEM_PROMPT + build_pair_prompt template (lines 62-103); a future template change would bump to v2.

Cross-impact (analyzed before capture, prior turn):

- scripts/export.py: ZERO impact. export.py reads only nodes, edges, chunk_embeddings — never staged_edges. Confirmed via grep. Adding columns to staged_edges is invisible to the export.
- schema/corpus-schema.sql (the published Postgres schema mirrored in guru-web): ZERO impact. The published `edges` table doesn't carry model attribution, and we are NOT proposing to propagate model into live edges or the export. Provenance lives in staging only.
- scripts/auto_promote_edges.py: reads staged_edges to promote into live edges. Currently copies (source, target, edge_type, tier, justification). Does NOT need to copy model into edges (since live edges don't carry it), but a follow-up could add a "promote only edges from model X" filter if useful. Out of scope here.
- scripts/review_edges.py: presents staged_edges rows for human review. May benefit from showing the model alongside each proposal (informational), but not required for the swap to function.

Files to touch:
- scripts/schema.sql — add columns + new UNIQUE index on staged_edges
- scripts/propose_edges.py — update upsert_staged_edge() signature + INSERT, swap --model default
- scripts/migrations/ — new migration file that ALTERs staged_edges + UPDATEs the historical rows with the Mistral backfill values
- (Optional) tests covering the new INSERT path and the migration

Verification after implementing:
- ALTER + UPDATE applied: `sqlite3 data/guru.db "SELECT model, COUNT(*) FROM staged_edges GROUP BY model"` shows all existing rows attributed to Mistral.
- Run propose_edges.py against a small slice (e.g. one --tradition) with the new default. Confirm new rows in staged_edges have non-null model + prompt_version.
- Confirm UNIQUE constraint allows the same (source, target) pair to appear with different model values.

resolution.note: v3_005 migration adds (model, prompt_version) to staged_edges with partial UNIQUE on pending rows; backfilled 5966 historical rows with Mistral-Small-3.2-24B-Instruct-2506-UD-Q5_K_XL.gguf / v1. propose_edges.py default model swapped to Mistral, upsert writes provenance on every INSERT. schema.sql and 4 inline test fixtures updated to match. 177 tests pass.
